us-politics.headline: Trump Escalates Iran Threats as Military Rescue Succeeds
us-politics.body:
Trump threatened Iran with 'Hell' in a profane Easter post if Tehran blocks the Strait of Hormuz, while declining to rule out ground troops if no deal is reached. A downed US airman was rescued behind enemy lines with CIA assistance - Iran says US aircraft were destroyed in the operation. Netanyahu called Trump to congratulate him on the rescue, signalling close coordination.

Republicans are divided on next steps. One House member says boots on the ground would only be justified to seize enriched uranium. Another says a ground invasion is unlikely. Democrats are louder in opposition - Rep. Adam Smith called a potential Iran war a costly mistake, and Rep. Auchincloss argued Iran's grip on the Strait is more dangerous than its nuclear programme.

A former Trump adviser warned the threatening rhetoric will kill any chance of a deal.

sources.title: Netanyahu says he spoke with Trump, congratulated him on rescuing downed pilot
Iran says US aircraft destroyed during recovery mission for missing service member
Rep. Madeleine Dean discusses Iran and President Trump's proposed budget
House Republican says he doesn’t think ground troops will be needed to open the Strait of Hormuz
Marjorie Taylor Greene rails against ‘insane’ Trump after Easter Iran threat
Top Armed Services Democrat says Iran war ‘mistake in large part because of the cost’
Trump declines to rule out ground troops if Iran doesn’t make a deal
Trump threatens Iran with ‘Hell’ over Strait of Hormuz in profane post
House Republican: ‘Only purpose’ for US boots on the ground in Iran is ‘to get the enriched uranium’
Former Trump adviser on president’s latest Iran threat: ‘Not going to get a deal’
House Democrat: Iran’s control of Strait of Hormuz ‘more strategically vital’ than nuclear weapon
Inside the daring rescue of airman behind enemy lines: How CIA assisted with 'deception campaign'

us-politics.headline: DHS Funding Lapses as GOP Divisions Stall Congress
us-politics.body:
The Department of Homeland Security has lost funding after House Republicans failed to act before recess, stalled by internal divisions and what sources describe as Trump's detachment from the negotiations. House Minority Leader Jeffries publicly called on Speaker Johnson to recall members from recess to resolve the lapse.

The standoff reflects a broader Republican governing problem - the party controls both chambers but cannot consistently deliver legislation. Democrats are watching closely, with some analysts arguing the affordability failures of the current moment could hand them an opening in the midterms if they sharpen their economic message.

sources.title: Republican divisions, Trump’s detachment stymie GOP efforts to reopen DHS
Jeffries calls on Johnson to end House recess amid DHS funding lapse
‘Affordability’ could eventually bite Democrats in the bum

us-politics.headline: Bondi Out, Trump Promises No Further Cabinet Shake-Up
us-politics.body:
Trump fired Attorney General Pam Bondi and DHS Secretary Kristi Noem, then told reporters not to expect further Cabinet changes. Saturday Night Live mocked Bondi's departure in its cold open. Sen. Tim Kaine said her firing should serve as a warning to whoever takes the job next.

Marjorie Taylor Greene broke with Trump publicly, calling his Easter Iran threat 'insane' - a rare rebuke from one of his most loyal allies. The episode underscores growing tension between Trump's impulses and the limits of his congressional base.

sources.title: SNL rips Bondi’s departure in cold open
Marjorie Taylor Greene rails against ‘insane’ Trump after Easter Iran threat
Trump says to not expect Cabinet shake-up after Bondi, Noem firings
Kaine: Bondi firing ‘should be a lesson’ for next attorney general nominee

us-politics.headline: Democrats Eye 2028 as Party Debates Strategy and Outreach
us-politics.body:
Arizona Rep. Ruben Gallego said he is open to a 2028 presidential run, telling reporters 'we have to look at it.' Rep. Ro Khanna argued Democrats who refuse to engage with non-traditional media figures like streamer Hasan Piker are making an electoral mistake, saying that approach 'will cost us future elections.'

The internal debate reflects a party still searching for its footing after 2024. Maryland Governor Wes Moore added an economic dimension, arguing that ending foreign wars is the most effective way to bring down gas prices - a line that blends anti-war politics with kitchen-table concerns.

sources.title: Khanna: Those saying ‘don’t engage’ with personalities like Hasan Piker ‘will cost us future elections’
Gallego on possible 2028 presidential run: ‘We have to look at it’
Moore: Ending foreign wars ‘best thing that we could do’ to lower gas prices

au-politics.headline: Moira Deeming Secures Top Victorian Liberal Senate Spot
au-politics.body:
Moira Deeming has won preselection for the top position on the Victorian Liberal Senate ticket, ending a bitter internal battle within the state party. Deeming, a conservative MP who has clashed repeatedly with Liberal leadership over social policy, now has a strong platform heading into the next federal election cycle.

Her victory reflects ongoing tension between the Liberal Party's moderate and conservative wings in Victoria. Supporters argue she represents grassroots members; critics within the party have previously sought to expel her. The outcome signals that conservative factions currently hold the upper hand in Victorian Liberal preselection contests.

sources.title: Australia news live: Moira Deeming secures top spot on Victorian Liberal ticket; Chris Bowen calls for Hormuz reopening
Moira Deeming wins Victorian Liberal preselection battle

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Drives Australians Toward EVs and E-Bikes
au-politics.body:
Soaring fuel prices are reshaping how Australians commute and buy vehicles. E-bike retailers report a surge in sales as drivers abandon cars to cut costs, while the secondhand electric vehicle market is tightening, with used EV prices rising as demand outpaces supply.

Petrol station owners are also feeling the squeeze, with some questioning whether running a servo remains viable under current price pressures. Energy Minister Chris Bowen has called for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, pointing to global supply disruption as a key driver of domestic fuel costs. The crisis is accelerating a shift in consumer behaviour that policy alone had struggled to achieve.

sources.title: Australia news live: Moira Deeming secures top spot on Victorian Liberal ticket; Chris Bowen calls for Hormuz reopening
Commuters are switching from cars to e-bikes to save money on fuel
Price crunch has some servo owners asking: 'Is it really worth doing this?'
‘Doesn’t make sense to hold on to a combustion engine’: used EV prices rise as Australia’s fuel crisis hits

au-politics.headline: Human Rights and Indigenous Justice Issues Dominate Domestic News
au-politics.body:
The Australian Human Rights Commission is investigating the NSW government over allegations of racial discrimination, a case that adds pressure to state authorities already facing scrutiny over their treatment of minority communities. Separately, a family court has ordered the return of an Aboriginal child moved 1,700 kilometres from their remote Northern Territory community, ruling the relocation unjustified.

A youth justice watchdog has also called for urgent upgrades to detention cells it describes as harmful, warning current conditions undermine access to justice for young people. Taken together, the cases point to persistent systemic concerns around race, child welfare and detention standards across multiple Australian jurisdictions.

sources.title: Australian Human Rights Commission investigates NSW government over allegations of racial discrimination
Watchdog seeks upgrade to 'harmful' youth cells to improve access to justice
Aboriginal child moved 1,700km from remote NT community should be returned, family court rules

au-politics.headline: Middle East Tensions Escalate as Trump Threatens Iran Strikes
au-politics.body:
Donald Trump has threatened to bomb Iranian power plants in an expletive-laden social media post, raising fears of direct US military involvement in the Middle East conflict. US intelligence agencies have separately alleged that China is sharing satellite imagery of American military bases with Iran, deepening the geopolitical stakes.

Israel has continued strikes in Lebanon, with reports of attacks on Christian villages that have no affiliation with Hezbollah. Analysts warn Trump's shifting public statements make any ceasefire timeline unreliable. Australia's government is watching developments closely given the fuel price impact of Hormuz disruption on domestic consumers.

sources.title: Australia news live: Moira Deeming secures top spot on Victorian Liberal ticket; Chris Bowen calls for Hormuz reopening
Trump's latest tirade shows timeframes 'all but meaningless'
Live: Trump threatens to bomb Iran power plants in expletive-laden post
Christian villages in Lebanon with no affiliation to Hezbollah attacked by Israel
US intelligence says Chinese satellite imagery of bases is helping Iran

world.headline: US-Iran War Escalates Near Hormuz as Trump Issues Threats
world.body:
The conflict between the United States and Iran intensified sharply, with Trump posting an expletive-laden social media threat warning Iran against blocking the Strait of Hormuz. BBC reporters near the strait described the area as the edge of a battlefield, underscoring how close the region is to broader maritime confrontation.

Trump declared victory after a US airman was rescued from a remote part of Iran, but analysts warn significant threats to American operations remain. Iran's internet has been shut down for a record-breaking stretch under wartime conditions, fuelling civilian frustration. Iranian missiles struck a residential building in Haifa and an industrial area in southern Israel, while OPEC+ moved to hike oil output, cautioning that recovery from the attacks will be slow.

sources.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
'We are at the edge of a battlefield': BBC reports near Strait of Hormuz
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Analysis: Trump declares victory in Iran war after rescue, but threats to US operation still loom
Trump threatens Iran in expletive-filled social media post
Frustration grows as Iran’s wartime internet shutdown breaks grim record
Video captures Iranian missile striking residential building in Haifa
OPEC+ agrees to hike oil output, warns of slow recovery after attacks
Iranian missile strikes industrial area in southern Israel

world.headline: Israel Strikes Gaza, Lebanon and Iran Simultaneously
world.body:
Israel carried out strikes across multiple fronts, killing at least 14 people in Lebanon and hitting a crowd in Gaza City. Hamas's armed wing flatly rejected disarmament demands, calling them unacceptable while Israeli attacks continued. Christians in Gaza marked Easter under bombardment, refusing to celebrate.

Conditions inside Gaza's tent camps are deteriorating rapidly, with rodent infestations spreading alongside the ongoing military assault. Far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir outlined plans for a Palestinian death row, drawing fresh condemnation. The Pope warned on Easter Sunday that the world is growing indifferent to violence.

sources.title: Has the humanitarian crisis in Gaza been ignored?
Hamas armed wing says disarmament demands not acceptable
Israeli air strike hits crowd in Gaza City
Hamas armed wing rejects disarmament while Israel continues attacks
Israel continues deadly attacks on Gaza while striking Iran, Lebanon
Rodent infestations and attacks spread in Gaza tent camps
‘Refuse to celebrate’: Christians in Gaza mark sombre Easter amid genocide
Israeli air strikes, shelling across Lebanon add to rising death toll
At least 14 people killed in Israeli strikes across Lebanon
Pope warns world is becoming ‘indifferent’ to violence
Israel’s Ben-Gvir details plans for Palestinian ‘death row’

world.headline: Explosives Found at Russian Gas Pipeline Across Two Countries
world.body:
Serbia and Hungary both reported discovering explosives planted at a Russian gas pipeline, raising alarm across Central Europe. Hungary separately alleged a plot to blow up the pipeline ahead of its upcoming election, framing the incident as a politically motivated attack.

The coordinated nature of the finds across two borders points to a deliberate operation rather than an isolated incident. No group has claimed responsibility. The discovery adds pressure to an already volatile European energy security landscape.

sources.title: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
Serbia, Hungary say explosives found at Russian gas pipeline

world.headline: Dozens Missing After Migrant Boat Capsizes Near Libya
world.body:
A boat carrying migrants capsized in the Mediterranean near Libya, leaving dozens missing. Search and rescue operations were under way, though the death toll remained unclear. The crossing remains one of the world's deadliest migration routes.

In a separate incident, Nigeria's army rescued 31 hostages following an attack on a church, with military forces moving quickly after the assault. And in the UK, police arrested seven protesters near an RAF base used by US forces, amid growing demonstrations over American military activity on British soil.

sources.title: Nigeria's army rescues 31 hostages after church attack
UK police arrest seven protesters near RAF base used by US
Dozens missing after boat carrying migrants capsizes near Libya
Dozens missing after migrant boat capsizes in Mediterranean

ai.headline: AI Music Tools Face Copyright Chaos From Both Sides
ai.body:
Suno, the AI music generator, is generating legal headaches as musicians discover the platform produces near-identical copies of their work. One artist reports the company is not only cloning her music but filing copyright claims against her own original recordings - flipping the victim and perpetrator in the same dispute.

The chaos cuts both ways. Italian broadcaster RAI copyright-struck Nvidia over footage Nvidia itself created to demonstrate DLSS 5, its own graphics technology. The claim was baseless and quickly exposed, but it illustrates how automated copyright enforcement systems are misfiring badly as AI-generated and AI-adjacent content floods the internet. Neither creators nor corporations have reliable tools to sort out who owns what anymore.

sources.title: Italian TV Copyright-Strikes Nvidia over Nvidia's Own DLSS 5 Footage
Musician says AI company is cloning her music, filing claims against her
Suno is a music copyright nightmare

ai.headline: Microsoft Calls Copilot Entertainment, Not a Serious Tool
ai.body:
Microsoft's terms of service now describe Copilot as being "for entertainment purposes only" - a striking disclaimer for a product the company has aggressively marketed as a productivity revolution and embedded across its entire software suite. The language appears designed to limit liability for bad outputs, but it hands critics a sharp line of attack.

The admission lands alongside a broader critique that Microsoft has lacked a coherent software strategy for decades. A widely-read post argues the company has drifted without a clear GUI vision since Charles Petzold's era of Windows programming. Together, the two stories paint a picture of a company selling AI ambition while quietly hedging its bets in the fine print.

sources.title: Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of use
Microsoft hasn't had a coherent GUI strategy since Petzold

ai.headline: Google's Gemma 4 Runs on iPhones and Local Machines
ai.body:
Google's Gemma 4 model is now available on iPhone through the AI Edge Gallery app, and developers are running it locally via LM Studio's new headless CLI alongside Claude Code. The combination lets engineers spin up capable models on consumer hardware without cloud dependencies. That matters for privacy, cost, and latency.

Meanwhile, Qwen-3.6-Plus crossed one trillion tokens processed in a single day on OpenRouter, a milestone that signals just how fast inference demand is scaling. The race is no longer only about which model scores highest on benchmarks - it is about which ones can actually handle the volume.

sources.title: Qwen-3.6-Plus is the first model to break 1T tokens processed in a day
Gemma 4 on iPhone
Running Gemma 4 locally with LM Studio's new headless CLI and Claude Code

ai.headline: Japan's Labor Shortage Is Robotics' Biggest Opportunity
ai.body:
Japan is deploying physical AI robots not to displace workers but to fill roles that have gone chronically unfilled - elder care, logistics, and manual labor in an aging, shrinking workforce. The country's demographic crisis is proving to be a forcing function that is accelerating real-world robotics deployment faster than any venture capital thesis has managed elsewhere.

The results are early but concrete. Robots are operating in environments that were considered too unstructured for automation just a few years ago. Japan is showing that necessity, not novelty, is what finally moves experimental technology out of the lab.

sources.title: In Japan, the robot isn't coming for your job; it's filling the one nobody wants
In Japan, the robot isn’t coming for your job; it’s filling the one nobody wants

btc.headline: Polymarket Pulls Iran Markets After Backlash, Odds Hit 63%
btc.body:
Prediction market Polymarket removed a set of Iran-related markets following intense user backlash, days after Trump's posts pushed odds of a US invasion of Iran to 63% for 2026. The controversy centered on markets framed around potential military rescue scenarios, which critics called exploitative and irresponsible.

The episode highlights a recurring tension in prediction markets: the line between useful forecasting and profiting from geopolitical crisis. Polymarket's decision to pull the markets suggests the platform is sensitive to reputational risk, even as its Iran invasion odds remain live and widely cited.

sources.title: Polymarket odds of US invading Iran this year reach 63% after Trump's post
Polymarket pulls controversial Iran rescue markets after intense backlash

btc.headline: Bitcoin Front-Runs the Fed as ETF Money Changes the Game
btc.body:
Bitcoin is no longer reacting to Federal Reserve decisions - it is pricing them in ahead of time. Analysts point to the rise of spot ETFs as the driver, with institutional money moving on macro expectations rather than waiting for rate announcements to land.

A Bitcoin Policy Institute executive added a complementary argument this week, describing Bitcoin and the US dollar as having a symbiotic rather than adversarial relationship. Together, these views mark a shift in how serious market participants frame Bitcoin - less as a speculative hedge and more as a macro asset with its own leading indicators.

sources.title: Bitcoin and the US dollar have a 'symbiotic' relationship: BPI exec
Bitcoin is now front-running the Fed rather than reacting to it. ETFs are the cause

btc.headline: Quantum Computing and AI Are Closing In on Crypto Security
btc.body:
Two separate warnings landed this week about the long-term security of crypto infrastructure. CoinDesk published a plain-language explainer on quantum computing, making the case that sufficiently powerful quantum machines could eventually crack Bitcoin's cryptographic foundations - a threat that sounds distant but is moving closer.

Separately, Ledger's CTO warned that AI is already making crypto's security problems worse today, lowering the cost and skill required to execute sophisticated attacks. The combination of near-term AI threats and longer-horizon quantum risk paints a challenging picture for an industry that has historically treated security as an afterthought.

sources.title: A simple explainer on what quantum computing actually is, and why it is terrifying for bitcoin
AI is making crypto's security problem even worse, Ledger CTO warns

btc.headline: Drift Incident Could Trigger Civil Negligence Claims, Attorney Says
btc.body:
A crypto attorney told CoinTelegraph that the recent Drift incident may meet the legal threshold for civil negligence, a notable escalation in how the industry's security failures are being framed legally. If users suffered losses due to inadequate safeguards, the argument goes, the platform could face liability rather than simply reputational damage.

The case is one to watch. Civil negligence claims against crypto platforms remain rare, but a successful action could set a precedent that forces protocols to treat security obligations more like traditional financial institutions do.

sources.title: Crypto attorney says Drift incident may qualify as 'civil negligence'

food.name: Miso-Glazed Cabbage Steaks
food.description: Thick-cut cabbage roasted until caramelised and lacquered with a sweet-salty miso and mirin glaze.
food.tip: Press the cabbage slices dry before roasting and use a cast-iron pan — the direct contact heat is what creates the deep, almost meaty char on the cut face.

exercise.name: Monday Reset: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy shopping bags, a clear corridor or outdoor path
exercise.description: Alternate between farmer's carries, suitcase carries (one side only), and overhead carries for 40-metre lengths, resting only when you set the weight down — brutally simple full-body conditioning that most people never try.

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity Over Time
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had every component replaced remains fundamentally the same thing — and when applied to human cells, memories, and beliefs, it becomes a sharp question about whether the 'you' of ten years ago is the same person sitting here now.
learn.why: On a Monday at the start of a new week it's a quietly destabilising and productive thought experiment — philosophers from Heraclitus to Derek Parfit have taken it seriously, and spending an hour with Parfit's 'Reasons and Persons' chapter on personal identity will change how you think about continuity, responsibility, and change.
